hypothesis: If a frozen large language model is steered during molecular generation by a Concept Bottleneck Model that learns property-specific embedding vectors and injects a weighted steering vector into the model's hidden activations, then the generated molecules will more accurately achieve the targeted property values (lower MAE for QED and LogP) while preserving validity, novelty, and synthesizability, and will outperform vanilla LLMs and baselines, including under out-of-distribution conditions.
